stem: A secondary school has a network in its building and also connects to other schools in the same academy trust across the country.
model_answer: A LAN (Local Area Network) covers a small geographic area such as a single site; the hardware is usually owned by the organisation and the network is joined by cables or Wi-Fi on the premises. A WAN (Wide Area Network) connects sites over a large geographic area (often between cities or countries) using infrastructure that is typically leased from telecoms providers rather than owned outright. The school network is a LAN; the connection between schools across the trust forms a WAN.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Compare a LAN with a WAN. You should refer to geographic coverage and ownership of the infrastructure.

mark_points.text: a LAN covers a small geographic area (one building/site)
a WAN covers a large geographic area (between cities/countries)
LAN infrastructure is usually owned by the organisation
WAN infrastructure is typically leased/rented from telecoms providers
